workflows.title: Omnibound v12 — Signal-Led Outbound
workflows.description: Production multi-variant cold outbound system at Omnibound AI. News hook IIFE classifies 6 trigger types (hire, funding, launch, expansion, partnership, acquisition) with a 30-day recency gate. E1 x 4 signal variants + E2 x 3 + E3 x 3 body variants. 14 subject line variants rotated by company charCode hash. 1.5%+ reply rates across 300+ mailboxes.
workflows.author: Rasul Shaikh
steps: Pull news signals via Trigify — classify as hire, funding, launch, expansion, partnership, or acquisition
Run NEWS HOOK FORMULA (Clay IIFE) — 30-day recency gate, outputs one-line personalized hook by news type
Select email variant: E1 (content investment, category expansion, funding/growth, or no signal), E2 (3 variants), or E3 (3 variations)
Pick subject line from 14 variants — pseudo-random rotation via company charCode hash for even distribution
Gate on col 60 PASS before Smartlead send — blocks non-qualifying contacts
Push reply context to HubSpot with signal routing — weekly deliverability audit loop

copy_preview.label: E1 — No Signal
copy_preview.subject: your seo pipeline moved to ai search
copy_preview.body:
Hey Aybike,

When operations teams evaluate last mile delivery software in AI search engines, Locus shows up. DispatchTrack doesn't. That's pipeline going to Locus instead of DispatchTrack.

When buyers evaluate last mile delivery software vendors, they're typing very specific questions into AI search engines. Are you building content around those exact prompts, or around keywords from generic AI visibility tools?

The difference shows up in pipeline. Brands showing up in the right AI answers aren't guessing what buyers search — they're working from real buyer conversations and market context.

That's exactly what Omnibound surfaces and builds content from.

Worth 30 minutes to see what that looks like for your last mile delivery software?

copy_preview.label: E1 — Funding Signal
copy_preview.subject: saw the raise. pipeline velocity matters now.
copy_preview.body:
Hey Aybike,

Saw the DispatchTrack DT WMS launch. That's exactly the kind of proof point that captures pipeline when last mile delivery software buyers compare vendors in AI search.

Post-raise, pipeline targets just got bigger. Most teams respond by scaling content. The ones building pipeline through AI search aren't just publishing more — they're building from the exact prompts buyers type when evaluating last mile delivery software vendors.

That's the difference between content that earns weak mentions and content that drives inbound consistently.

Omnibound builds from that intelligence. Worth a 30-minute conversation?

copy_preview.label: E2 — Buyer Intelligence
copy_preview.subject: locus shows up. dispatchtrack doesn't.
copy_preview.body:
Hey Aybike,

DispatchTrack's deal conversations hold the exact questions operations teams ask when evaluating last mile delivery software vendors — the objections, the outcomes, the proof points. Locus figured out how to turn that into pipeline. DispatchTrack hasn't yet.

Omnibound extracts those prompts from sales calls, CRM notes, and market signals, and builds content that puts DispatchTrack where Locus is — so operations teams find DispatchTrack when they're building their shortlist.

Can I show you how this works in 30 minutes?

copy_preview.label: E3 — Decision Influence
copy_preview.subject: while your content sits on the sidelines
copy_preview.body:
Hey Aybike,

Getting cited in AI search is one thing. Influencing a decision is another. Locus is already shaping how operations teams think about last mile delivery software — the questions they ask, the criteria they set, the names they trust.

Citations built from real buyer conversations and market context answer the specific questions operations teams ask when evaluation criteria are forming. That's what moves pipeline.

Most content earns weak mentions because it was built from guessed prompts, not the language buyers use mid-evaluation.

Omnibound closes that gap. If this is on your radar, happy to connect.

workflows.title: Volume-Based Outbound Campaign
workflows.description: High-volume ICP-list campaign engine: Apollo pull, email waterfall (Findymail + Prospeo + ZeroBounce), deliverability-gated upload, A/B tested sequences across 250+ sending domains. Used at RemoteState to hit 95%+ deliverability and 12-20 SQLs per campaign run.
workflows.author: Rasul Shaikh
steps: Pull ICP list from Apollo — filters: title, headcount, industry, tech stack, geo
Email waterfall in Clay: Findymail → Prospeo → BetterContact → ZeroBounce verify
Run pre-send list gate (col 60 PASS) — blocks low-quality contacts before upload
A/B split sequences: subject line variants, opening line variants, CTA variants
Upload to Smartlead across 250+ sending domains — 50-domain rotation per campaign
Weekly deliverability audit — bounce rate, spam rate, domain health dashboard
Positive replies auto-routed to HubSpot via n8n webhook — lead score + Slack alert

copy_preview.label: E1 — Hiring Signal
copy_preview.subject: scaling eng at {{company}}
copy_preview.body:
Hey {{first_name}},

Saw {{company}} has {{open_roles}}+ engineering roles open. Most companies at that pace hit a bottleneck — not enough qualified bench, too slow to vet.

RemoteState deploys pre-vetted engineers in 14 days. Same timezone, flexible contracts, 95%+ 6-month retention across 40+ deployments in {{industry}}.

15 minutes to see if the model fits your hiring pipeline?

copy_preview.label: E2 — Follow-up
copy_preview.subject: re: scaling eng at {{company}}
copy_preview.body:
Hey {{first_name}},

Following up on the note about engineering deployment.

Most IT services pitches lead with CVs. We lead with deployment speed — the median time from brief to engineer on your codebase is 11 days.

Would a quick call make sense this week?

workflows.title: Niche / Sub-Niche Campaign
workflows.description: Vertical-specific outbound targeting deep sub-niches with industry-native messaging. Built for FalconWise (MENA enterprise cybersecurity: CISOs, CTOs, Heads of Security) and RemoteState (US/EU enterprise IT services). 5-source enrichment for sub-niche ICP accuracy, 45% improvement in targeting precision.
workflows.author: Rasul Shaikh
steps: Define niche ICP: vertical → sub-vertical → pain cluster (e.g. cybersecurity → OT/ICS security → compliance gaps)
5-source enrichment: Apollo + Persana + Clearbit + GPT + internal CRM for sub-niche fit score
Segment by sub-niche pain — build separate sequences per pain cluster, not per persona
Write industry-native copy: use the language the niche actually uses, not generic GTM speak
Co-marketing layer: identify niche-adjacent partners for warm intro campaigns
Run sequences on Smartlead — dedicated domain pool per vertical to protect domain reputation
Tag niche in HubSpot — track SQL conversion rate per sub-vertical to double down on winners

copy_preview.label: MENA Cybersecurity — CISO
copy_preview.subject: AI search is changing how CISOs evaluate vendors in {{industry}}
copy_preview.body:
Hey {{first_name}},

{{industry}} buyers in the Gulf are running AI searches before booking demos — the same way they used to Google vendors.

{{competitor}} is showing up in those answers. {{company}} isn't yet.

FalconWise maps the exact prompts your buyers type — from compliance questions to vendor comparisons — and builds content that puts {{company}} in those answers before the shortlist forms.

25 of the 30 enterprise security teams we worked with saw AI-referred inbound within 60 days.

Worth 20 minutes?

copy_preview.label: IT Services — Head of Engineering
copy_preview.subject: {{company}}'s hiring pace vs. deployment speed
copy_preview.body:
Hey {{first_name}},

IT services outbound in {{industry}} is mostly noise — generic staffing pitches that ignore how your engineering org actually works.

RemoteState approaches it differently: we map your stack, your sprint cycles, and your bottleneck — then match engineers who've shipped in that exact environment.

40+ deployments across US/EU {{industry}} companies. Median time to productive: 11 days.

15 minutes to see the match rate for {{company}}'s current roles?

workflows.title: Relevance-Based Campaign
workflows.description: Content and intent-triggered outreach based on topic relevance signals: GA4 content engagement, GSC search query data, Trigify job change signals, and AI search gap analysis. Fires when a buyer's content behaviour indicates active evaluation. Used at Omnibound AI to shorten qualification from 48h to 4h.
workflows.author: Rasul Shaikh
steps: Capture relevance signals: GA4 content engagement + GSC query data + Trigify job change + RB2B anonymous visit
Score relevance composite in Clay: topic match × recency × engagement depth × ICP fit
Route by relevance tier: T1 (direct outreach same day) / T2 (sequence D1+D3+D7) / T3 (nurture only)
Generate relevance-specific copy hook in Claude: reference the exact content or topic that triggered the signal
Gate on col 60 PASS before send — blocks low-relevance contacts that slipped through scoring
Push triggered contact + relevance context to HubSpot — deal stage auto-updated on reply
Mine weekly: which relevance triggers converted to SQL — feed back into signal scoring weights

copy_preview.label: Content Engagement Trigger
copy_preview.subject: {{company}} is missing pipeline from {{category}} searches
copy_preview.body:
Hey {{first_name}},

{{company}}'s content is getting traction. It's not converting into pipeline.

The gap: buyers searching AI for {{category}} vendors aren't finding {{company}} in the answers — they're finding {{competitor}}.

Omnibound identifies which buyer prompts your content almost answers, then closes the gap so {{company}} shows up when purchase intent is highest.

3 clients saw qualified pipeline from AI-referred contacts within 45 days of deployment.

Open to a 20-minute walkthrough?

copy_preview.label: Job Change Signal (New Buyer)
copy_preview.subject: {{first_name}} — congrats on the new role at {{company}}
copy_preview.body:
Hey {{first_name}},

Congratulations on the move to {{company}}.

New {{title}} roles in {{industry}} usually inherit a pipeline problem — especially in the first 90 days when you're mapping what's working and what isn't.

Omnibound helps {{category}} teams capture pipeline from AI search that traditional SEO misses. Usually takes one session to show where {{company}}'s content is leaving buyers on the table.

Worth 20 minutes this week?

workflows.title: Hyper-Personalised Campaign
workflows.description: Account-level research pipeline: 7-stage LLM workflow in Clay (role map → SEO intel → gap analysis → AI search gap → strategic positioning → email hook → col 60 gate). Per-contact custom first lines generated from firmographic + technographic + behavioral composite. Replaces 6+ hrs/batch manual research with sub-30-min automated pass at Omnibound AI.
workflows.author: Rasul Shaikh
steps: Role map: pull title, seniority, team size, reporting line — build ICP fit score per contact
SEO intel pull via SEMrush/Ahrefs API in Clay: organic traffic, top pages, competitor gap
Gap analysis: identify where prospect ranks below competitor on buyer-intent keywords
AI search gap: test which prompts buyer types in GPT/Perplexity return competitor but not prospect
Strategic positioning: Claude generates account-specific positioning angle based on gap + ICP role
Email hook generation: per-contact custom first line referencing the specific gap found
Col 60 gate: blocks contacts where personalisation score is below threshold — no generic sends

copy_preview.label: SEMrush Stack Displacement
copy_preview.subject: {{company}}'s SEO vs {{competitor}}'s AI visibility gap
copy_preview.body:
Hey {{first_name}},

Ran {{company}}'s content through our AI search diagnostic against {{competitor}}.

{{competitor}} shows up in 14 of the 20 prompts your {{buyer_role}} buyers type when evaluating {{category}} vendors. {{company}} shows up in 3.

The gap isn't domain authority — it's that {{competitor}}'s content is built from buyer conversations. {{company}}'s is built from keyword tools.

Omnibound pulls those conversations from your CRM and builds content that closes that gap.

15 minutes to show you the specific prompts you're missing?

copy_preview.label: AI Search Gap (No Competitor Named)
copy_preview.subject: how {{category}} buyers find vendors before booking demos
copy_preview.body:
Hey {{first_name}},

Buyers evaluating {{category}} vendors are asking AI search engines for recommendations before they talk to sales.

I mapped the top 20 prompts your buyers use. {{company}} shows up in fewer than 4 of them.

The companies that own those answers are already shaping how your buyers think about the category — before your team gets in the room.

Omnibound builds content from the exact language your buyers use when they're in evaluation mode.

Worth 30 minutes to see what that looks like for {{company}}?

workflows.title: Clay Webhook-to-HubSpot Enrichment
workflows.description: Product sign-up webhook fires into Clay, enriches contact and company in real-time, classifies email type (personal/work/role), scores via AI rubric (0-100), syncs tiered result to HubSpot with routing context. Qualification: 48h to under 4 hours.
workflows.author: Rasul Shaikh
steps: Email type classify: personal / work / role-based
HubSpot sync with tier + routing context
Tiered outreach: T1 HeyReach, T2-T3 Instantly

workflows.title: Pre-Send List Quality Gate
workflows.description: Grade any lead list A+ to F across 8 dimensions before Smartlead upload. n8n blocks below grade B and alerts Slack.
workflows.author: Rasul Shaikh

workflows.title: Weekly Deliverability Audit
workflows.description: SPF, DKIM, DMARC per domain, Smartlead inbox health, 1% reply rule. Cron Monday, Slack report.
workflows.author: Rasul Shaikh

workflows.title: Founder-Led Content Loop
workflows.description: Capture LinkedIn engagers, score Hot/Warm/Nurture, warm outbound referencing their interaction, mine calls for next post.
workflows.author: Rasul Shaikh

workflows.title: NeevCloud Content Quality Gate
workflows.description: Six-check gate for 80 posts/week: near-duplicate, cannibalisation, structure, readability, claims, confidentiality. PASS/FLAG/FAIL before CMS.
workflows.author: Rasul Shaikh

workflows.title: Signal-Led Email Cadences
workflows.description: 23 YAML cadences: trigger signals, persona, vertical, stage. MiniMax generates personalized hook per lead.

workflows.title: AI Lead Scorer
workflows.description: Score lead lists 0-100 with Claude. Firmographic, technographic, behavioral composite for tier routing.

workflows.description: Rasul-owned messaging system: first line (relevancy/observation/recency), 1-4 body blocks, single CTA, optional P.S., 80-word gate. personalizer + 23 cadences + Clay formulas + col 60 PASS.

workflows.description: Playwright scrape plus Claude. One hyper-personalized email per company URL in under 30 seconds.

workflows.description: Six production IIFEs: lead scoring, ICP fit, email quality, categorization, personalization hooks, reply intent.

workflows.title: Clay 9-Step Enrichment (90%+ Coverage)
workflows.description: Upload, company enrich, people enrich, email waterfall, verification, phone, Claygent custom data, scoring, CRM export. 85%+ email coverage vs 40% single finder.
workflows.author: Rasul Shaikh

workflows.title: 6 Buying Signals (Ranked by Correlation)
workflows.description: Former customers, new leadership, high-intent website, tech stack change, expansion, hiring/downsizing. Prioritize outreach by signal strength.
workflows.author: Rasul Shaikh

workflows.title: Bad Reviews Targeting Play
workflows.description: Scrape negative G2/Capterra reviews, reference their pain, offer alternatives. Outreach within days of review.
workflows.author: Rasul Shaikh

workflows.title: Inbound Followers Play
workflows.description: Monitor daily LinkedIn followers, filter to ICP via Clay, outreach within 24-48h. 25-30% reply rate on warm intent.

workflows.description: 9 sub-skills: job changes, funding, hiring, website visitors, company events, tech changes, competitor signals, content engagement, multi-signal scoring. 35-40% reply rates on signal-led (production).
